summary: E4 Testing Complete: Policy Conflict Engine feature fully functional. All 34 backend tests pass. All 4 conflict types detected (action_conflict, gap, overlap, redundancy). Resolve workflow creates audit logs. 5th KPI card shows critical conflicts on Overview dashboard.

issues: Console warning: <span> cannot be a child of <option> - minor React hydration warning in filter dropdowns, does not affect functionality

passed_tests: Backend: All 34 pytest tests pass (including 4 new PolicyEngine tests)
Backend: GET /api/policy-engine/conflicts returns 9 conflicts with summary (total, by_type, by_severity, agents_impacted, policies_impacted)
Backend: Conflicts include all 4 types: action_conflict (1 critical), gap (4 high), overlap (3 high), redundancy (1 low)
Backend: POST /api/policy-engine/conflicts/{id}/resolve marks conflict resolved and creates audit log
Backend: GET /api/policy-engine/scan-history returns list of previous scans
Frontend: Policy Engine page loads at /dashboard/policy-engine with title, subtitle, Run Scan button, last scan timestamp
Frontend: Summary cards show: 9 Total Conflicts, 1 Critical, 7 High, 7 Agents Impacted
Frontend: Conflict cards show severity badge, type badge, regulation badges, policy pills, agent pills, recommendation box
Frontend: Filter dropdowns work: All Types filter, All Severities filter, Show Resolved toggle
Frontend: Mark as Resolved button opens dialog, confirm creates audit log
Frontend: Sidebar shows 'Policy Registry' (renamed from Policies), 'Policy Engine' (new with Zap icon)
Frontend: Overview dashboard shows 5th KPI card 'Policy Conflicts' with critical count (1)
seed_data_creation: Policy conflicts seeded via /app/backend/seed.py: 2 conflicting policies on Fraud Detection Engine (block vs auto enforcement on same conditions), policy assignments to AML Transaction Monitor, Credit Risk Assessor, SOX Internal Control Auditor creating overlap scenarios.
context_for_next_testing_agent: E4 Policy Conflict Engine complete. 9 conflicts detected: 1 action_conflict (critical, DORA), 4 gaps (high), 3 overlaps (high), 1 redundancy (low). The resolve endpoint requires dpo/admin role. Resolved conflicts are stored in 'resolved_conflicts' collection. Scan history stored in 'conflict_scans' collection. Overview dashboard now has 5 KPI cards (grid lg:grid-cols-5). Previous iterations: iteration_4.json (E1 SOX), iteration_5.json (E2 SOX Wizard), iteration_6.json (E3 DLgs262+Readiness).

conflict_types_verified.example: Fraud Detection Engine - Block Suspicious Transactions vs Auto-Process Low-Risk Transactions (block vs auto enforcement)
Customer Due Diligence Bot, DORA Incident Responder, KYC Verification Agent, Dirigente Preposto Assistant - high-risk agents without policy coverage
AML Transaction Monitor (GDPR), SOX Internal Control Auditor (SOX), Fraud Detection Engine (DORA) - multiple policies with different rule types
Block Suspicious Transactions and Auto-Process Low-Risk Transactions - identical conditions

ui_elements_verified: Policy Engine page title and subtitle
Run Scan button with last scan timestamp
Summary cards (Total Conflicts, Critical, High, Agents Impacted)
Conflict cards with badges, pills, and recommendations
Mark as Resolved button and dialog
